meta.title: Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: A deterministic end-of-day reflection tool traversing three psychological axes: Locus of Control, Contribution Orientation, and Radius of Concern.

nodes.text:
Good evening. Before the day slips away — let's spend a few minutes with it.

No right answers. No scores. Just honest questions.
If you had to give today a single word, what would it be?
options: Productive
Draining
Mixed
Frustrating
answer=Productive|Mixed:A1_Q1_HIGH
answer=Draining|Frustrating:A1_Q1_LOW

nodes.text: You said "{A1_OPEN.answer}" — something moved today. When a good moment happened, what made it possible?
options: I prepared or planned for it
I made a quick call in the moment
The right people showed up
Honestly, I got a bit lucky

nodes.text: You said "{A1_OPEN.answer}" — that sounds heavy. When things went sideways, what was your first move?
options: I looked for something I could control
I vented — then tried to problem-solve
I waited to see if it would sort itself out
I felt stuck and stayed there for a while
answer=I prepared or planned for it|I made a quick call in the moment:A1_REF_INT
answer=The right people showed up|Honestly, I got a bit lucky:A1_REF_MIXED
answer=I looked for something I could control|I vented — then tried to problem-solve:A1_REF_MIXED
answer=I waited to see if it would sort itself out|I felt stuck and stayed there for a while:A1_REF_EXT

nodes.text: Think of one moment today when something didn't go as expected. Where did your mind go first?
options: What can I do differently?
Why does this keep happening to me?
What's the next step forward?
Who dropped the ball here?
answer=What can I do differently?|What's the next step forward?:A1_REF_INT
answer=Why does this keep happening to me?|Who dropped the ball here?:A1_REF_EXT

nodes.text:
You stayed in the driver's seat today — even when the road got rough. That's not naivety. It's a choice to look for leverage where you actually have it.

Agency doesn't mean controlling outcomes. It means deciding how you respond.

Today sounds like it pulled you in different directions. Some moments you were steering, others you were just holding on.

That tension — between responding and reacting — is where most of us actually live. Noticing it is the first step.

Hard days pull attention outward — to what others did, what circumstances conspired, what didn't go to plan. That's honest.

But somewhere in today, you made at least one call — even if it was just deciding to keep going. What was it?
Now let's shift from how you handled things — to what you gave.
Think of the most memorable interaction you had today. What was your primary feeling going into it?
options: I wanted to help or add value
I wanted to be heard or recognized
I just needed to get through it
I wasn't sure what I was there to do
answer=I wanted to help or add value:A2_Q1_CONTRIB
answer=I wanted to be heard or recognized:A2_Q1_ENTITLE
answer=I just needed to get through it|I wasn't sure what I was there to do:A2_Q1_NEUTRAL

nodes.text: Good instinct. Did you actually follow through on it — or did something get in the way?
options: Yes, I helped in the way I intended
I tried, but I'm not sure it landed
I helped in a different way than I expected
Something got in the way

nodes.text: Did you get what you were hoping for from that interaction?
options: Yes — they acknowledged my work
Not really — I felt overlooked
I realized mid-way I didn't actually need that
It shifted — I ended up focusing on them instead

nodes.text: Even in moments like that — something gets done, or something gets given. Looking back, what actually happened in that interaction?
options: I ended up helping more than I expected
I mostly received — support, information, energy
It was purely transactional — both sides took what they needed
Nothing meaningful changed for either of us
answer=I ended up helping more than I expected:A2_Q2_CONTRIB
answer=I mostly received — support, information, energy|It was purely transactional — both sides took what they needed|Nothing meaningful changed for either of us:A2_Q2_ENTITLE

nodes.text: Was there a moment today you did something that wasn't strictly your job — but you did it anyway?
options: Yes, I helped a colleague who was struggling
Yes, I pointed something out that no one asked me to
Not really — I stuck to what was on my plate
I wanted to, but I was too stretched
answer=Yes, I helped a colleague who was struggling|Yes, I pointed something out that no one asked me to:A2_REF_CONTRIB
answer=Not really — I stuck to what was on my plate|I wanted to, but I was too stretched:A2_REF_MIXED

nodes.text: Honest question: at the end of today, did you feel the world owed you something — or that you owed the world something?
options: Honestly, I felt a bit owed
Neither — I felt pretty even
I felt I still had things to give
I hadn't thought about it that way
answer=Honestly, I felt a bit owed:A2_REF_ENTITLE
answer=Neither — I felt pretty even|I hadn't thought about it that way:A2_REF_MIXED
answer=I felt I still had things to give:A2_REF_CONTRIB
answer=Yes, I helped in the way I intended|I helped in a different way than I expected:A2_REF_CONTRIB
answer=I tried, but I'm not sure it landed|Something got in the way:A2_REF_MIXED
answer=Yes — they acknowledged my work|Not really — I felt overlooked:A2_Q2_ENTITLE
answer=I realized mid-way I didn't actually need that|It shifted — I ended up focusing on them instead:A2_REF_CONTRIB

nodes.text:
You showed up for someone else today — or for something bigger than just your own agenda. That's not small.

Contribution doesn't have to be dramatic. Sometimes it's just paying attention to what's needed, and meeting it.

Today you were probably both — giving in some moments, receiving in others. Most days are like that.

The question worth sitting with: where were you most alive in those interactions — when you were getting, or when you were giving?

Feeling owed is a signal worth examining — not to judge yourself, but to understand it. It usually means something you expected didn't arrive.

What was it you were actually hoping for today?
One more turn. Let's zoom out — from you and your interactions, to the wider world those interactions touched.
When you think about today's biggest challenge — who came to mind while you were in it?
options: Mostly just me — it was my problem
My immediate team
A specific person who was affected more than me
The customer or end user who'd eventually feel the impact
answer=Mostly just me — it was my problem:A3_Q1_SELF
answer=My immediate team:A3_Q1_TEAM
answer=A specific person who was affected more than me|The customer or end user who'd eventually feel the impact:A3_Q1_OTHER

nodes.text: That's fair — some problems are yours to carry. But did anyone else's situation cross your mind at any point today? Even briefly?
options: Yes — a colleague seemed to be struggling
Yes — I thought about how my work affects others downstream
Not really — I was heads-down in my own world
I was too overwhelmed to look up
answer=Yes — a colleague seemed to be struggling|Yes — I thought about how my work affects others downstream:A3_Q2_EXPAND
answer=Not really — I was heads-down in my own world|I was too overwhelmed to look up:A3_Q2_NARROW

nodes.text: You kept the team in view — that matters. Was there a specific person on the team whose experience you were tracking?
options: Yes — I noticed someone struggling and checked in
Yes — I adjusted what I was doing because of them
Not specifically — I just had the team in the background
I thought about the team but didn't act on it
answer=Yes — I noticed someone struggling and checked in|Yes — I adjusted what I was doing because of them:A3_REF_ALTO
answer=Not specifically — I just had the team in the background|I thought about the team but didn't act on it:A3_Q2_EXPAND

nodes.text: You were holding someone else's experience while navigating your own — that takes a kind of dual awareness. Did it change how you acted?
options: Yes — I made a decision differently because of it
Yes — I reached out or offered help
I noticed, but it didn't change anything I did
It actually made my own problem feel smaller
answer=Yes — I made a decision differently because of it|Yes — I reached out or offered help|It actually made my own problem feel smaller:A3_REF_ALTO
answer=I noticed, but it didn't change anything I did:A3_REF_MID

nodes.text: Even on hard days, we're connected to more than just our own story. If you could go back to one moment today — and do something small for someone else — what would it be?
options: Check in on a colleague I noticed seemed off
Thank someone I took for granted
Share something useful I learned
Just be more present in a conversation

nodes.text: Being in your own head all day isn't a flaw — sometimes the work just demands it. But when the pressure eases tomorrow, who's worth noticing?
options: A colleague who seems stretched
Someone whose contribution I've been ignoring
A person who's been quieter than usual
I'll need to look up to even know

nodes.text:
You held more than just yourself in mind today. That widened perspective — seeing the team, the colleague, the end user — is what separates someone who works in isolation from someone who works in contribution.

That radius of concern is one of the quietest forms of leadership.

You caught a glimpse of a wider frame today — even if you couldn't always act on it. That awareness is not nothing.

The gap between noticing and acting closes with time. Today you noticed. That's the start.

Some days you need all of yourself just to get through your own day. There's no shame in that — endurance is also a form of contribution.

But Maslow had it right: the deepest satisfaction we find is in service to something beyond ourselves. Tomorrow is another chance to look up.

Today you showed up as "{A1_OPEN.answer}" — and somewhere in that, you navigated choices, interactions, and moments of connection.

On agency: you leaned {axis1.dominant}.
On contribution: you leaned {axis2.dominant}.
On your circle of concern: you leaned {axis3.dominant}.

{summary_reflection}

Thank you for sitting with today instead of just leaving it behind.
See you tomorrow.

summary_reflections.internal_contribution_altrocentric: You moved through today with agency, gave more than you took, and kept others in your field of view. That's a rare combination — and it compounds.
summary_reflections.internal_contribution_mixed: You drove your own day and showed up for others. The wider circle is already in your awareness — it'll widen further.
summary_reflections.internal_contribution_self: You stayed in control of your own story today. The next growth edge is letting others into that story.
summary_reflections.internal_mixed_altrocentric: You were in the driver's seat and you kept others in view. The contribution piece is where tomorrow can deepen.
summary_reflections.internal_mixed_mixed: A well-rounded day — agency, some giving, some wider awareness. That's honest and real.
summary_reflections.internal_mixed_self: You controlled what you could, but today was mostly about your own lane. That's okay — just notice it.
summary_reflections.internal_entitlement_altrocentric: You were proactive and outward-looking, even if the contribution piece felt a little uneasy today. That tension is productive.
summary_reflections.internal_entitlement_mixed: You had agency today. The invitation is to direct some of that energy outward, toward what others need.
summary_reflections.internal_entitlement_self: Strong sense of agency — that's real. The next step is pairing it with generosity, rather than expectation.
summary_reflections.external_contribution_altrocentric: Even when you felt the current was working against you, you kept giving and kept others in mind. That's real strength.
summary_reflections.external_contribution_mixed: You gave today even when you felt little control. That generosity under pressure is worth acknowledging.
summary_reflections.external_contribution_self: You contributed where you could, even if agency felt out of reach. That still counts.
summary_reflections.external_mixed_altrocentric: The day may have felt out of your hands, but your awareness extended beyond yourself. That matters.
summary_reflections.external_mixed_mixed: A tough day on all fronts. Go easy on yourself — and come back tomorrow.
summary_reflections.external_mixed_self: Hard day. You stayed in it. Tomorrow, look for one thing — just one — you can control.
summary_reflections.external_entitlement_altrocentric: The outer world felt like it was working against you, and you wanted more back. Fair. But you still kept others in view.
summary_reflections.external_entitlement_mixed: A tough day can make anyone feel owed something. Notice where the tide turns.
summary_reflections.external_entitlement_self: Rough day. It happens. The question for tomorrow: what's the one thing within reach?
